task_completion.description: Attempt to import the fells_boundary.kml file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A GIS/GPS application is open and there is an attempt to open or import 'C:\workspace\data\fells_boundary.kml' (e.g., file dialog open navigating to the path), even if the file is missing.

task_completion.description: Rename the track to 'Fells Reservation Boundary'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The track properties or layer list shows a track named 'Fells Reservation Boundary'.

task_completion.description: Set the track display color to Magenta.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The track properties show the color set to Magenta, or a track is visibly colored Magenta on the map.

task_completion.description: Create a waypoint named 'South Entrance' at 42.438, -71.105.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A waypoint properties window or list shows the name 'South Entrance' with coordinates approximately at Lat 42.438, Lon -71.105.

task_completion.description: Export the track and waypoint as a GPX file to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export or save dialog showing the file type as GPX and the destination path as 'C:\workspace\output\patrol_boundary.gpx'.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical GIS/GPS application to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a GUI application (e.g., Google Earth, QGIS, Garmin BaseCamp) rather than a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's export functionality rather than manually writing the GPX file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Export dialog boxes or application menus are visible, rather than a text editor open with XML/GPX code.

integrity.description: The track and waypoint are grouped together before export.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application's sidebar or layer panel shows both the track and the waypoint under the same list, folder, or layer.
